verses.1: My son, do not forget my teaching, But have your heart comply with my commandments;
verses.2: For length of days and years of life And peace they will add to you.
verses.3: Do not let kindness and truth leave you; Bind them around your neck, Write them on the tablet of your heart.
verses.4: So you will find favor and a good reputation In the sight of God and man.
verses.5: Trust in the LORD with all your heart And do not lean on your own understanding.
verses.6: In all your ways acknowledge Him, And He will make your paths straight.
verses.7: Do not be wise in your own eyes; Fear the LORD and turn away from evil.
verses.8: It will be healing to your body And refreshment to your bones.
verses.9: Honor the LORD from your wealth, And from the first of all your produce;
verses.10: Then your barns will be filled with plenty, And your vats will overflow with new wine.
verses.11: My son, do not reject the discipline of the LORD Or loathe His rebuke,
verses.12: For whom the LORD loves He disciplines, Just as a father disciplines the son in whom he delights.
verses.13: Blessed is a person who finds wisdom, And one who obtains understanding.
verses.14: For her profit is better than the profit of silver, And her produce better than gold.
verses.15: She is more precious than jewels, And nothing you desire compares with her.
verses.16: Long life is in her right hand; In her left hand are riches and honor.
verses.17: Her ways are pleasant ways, And all her paths are peace.
verses.18: She is a tree of life to those who take hold of her, And happy are those who hold on to her.
verses.19: The LORD founded the earth by wisdom, He established the heavens by understanding.
verses.20: By His knowledge the ocean depths were burst open, And the clouds drip with dew.
verses.21: My son, see that they do not escape from your sight; Comply with sound wisdom and discretion,
verses.22: And they will be life to your soul And adornment to your neck.
verses.23: Then you will walk in your way securely, And your foot will not stumble.
verses.24: When you lie down, you will not be afraid; When you lie down, your sleep will be sweet.
verses.25: Do not be afraid of sudden danger, Nor of trouble from the wicked when it comes;
verses.26: For the LORD will be your confidence, And will keep your foot from being caught.
verses.27: Do not withhold good from those to whom it is due, When it is in your power to do it.
verses.28: Do not say to your neighbor, “Go, and come back, And tomorrow I will give it to you, ” When you have it with you.
verses.29: Do not devise harm against your neighbor, While he lives securely beside you.
verses.30: Do not contend with a person for no reason, If he has done you no harm.
verses.31: Do not envy a violent person, And do not choose any of his ways.
verses.32: For the devious are an abomination to the LORD; But He is intimate with the upright.
verses.33: The curse of the LORD is on the house of the wicked, But He blesses the home of the righteous.
verses.34: Though He scoffs at the scoffers, Yet He gives grace to the needy.
verses.35: The wise will inherit honor, But fools increase dishonor.
